hero_title_accent: Role-Based Access for Digital Asset Teams
hero_subtitle: SFTY helps VASPs and regulated businesses enforce approval permissions, maintain audit trails, and manage case workflows with institutional-grade role controls.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Approval control becomes unclear across teams
problem_description: Without consistent role-based permissions, compliance workflows risk unauthorized approvals or delayed decision-making. This creates gaps in audit trails and makes it harder for VASPs to maintain clear operational governance and defend compliance reviews.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY structures approval workflows by assigning controlled permissions, recording every decision and escalation with audit trails, so compliance teams maintain clear governance and consistent oversight across case management.
workflow_title: From role permissions to case closure
workflow_description: SFTY structures approval permissions to manage case reviews, decisions, escalations and closures while preserving audit logs for institutional compliance control.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another workflow tool. Provider-neutral control
why_sfty_description: SFTY delivers a provider-neutral layer that connects role-based permissions, approval decisions, and audit evidence into one operational compliance control environment.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports regulated VASPs with permissioned workflows that support audit-ready evidence, bank due diligence and enterprise deployment governance from day one.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How does SFTY manage role-based approval permissions workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how case approvals are controlled and routed
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit trails capture approval and escalation history
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY supports governance for compliance teams
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance control layer that connects provider outputs into structured workflows, cases, evidence, and decisions for regulated digital asset teams.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage approval permissions?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports role-based access and approval workflows to control who can approve, reject, escalate, or close cases within compliance operations.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit logs, decision records, case history, and evidence packages to support review readiness and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects and orchestrates outputs from KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule, and screening providers into a connected compliance layer without disrupting existing systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC or KYT providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but sits above them, turning their outputs into structured compliance reviews, case management, and audit-ready evidence.
final_title: Control case approvals with role-based access
final_description: Review how SFTY supports permission management, audit trails and structured workflows for approval, escalation and case closure.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
